frameworks.name: change point detection
frameworks.one_line: detecting moments when an evidence stream's generative state shifts

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation with adjustable timescales
frameworks.one_line: parallel accumulators with distinct integration windows over a shared stream

frameworks.name: model-based vs model-free decision analysis
frameworks.one_line: complementary characterization of the same choice behavior

frameworks.name: decision bound adjustment
frameworks.one_line: cue-driven modulation of threshold for committing to a change

core_question: How does knowledge about the space of possible changes shape human change point detection over a single evidence stream?
key_assumptions: observers can run multiple evidence-evaluation processes in parallel on one stream
informative cues convey the type of upcoming change
decision bounds can be set flexibly given cue information
manipulations: number and type of possible changes available
presence vs absence of informative cues about the change type
stochastic auditory evidence stream as the shared input
key_findings: subjects apply distinct timescales of evidence evaluation simultaneously to one stream
informative cues improve change point detection accuracy
improvements involve both timescale selection and decision bound shifts
interpretations: evidence evaluation supports multiple parallel timescales that can be rapidly adjusted
neural mechanisms must accommodate flexible timescales and information thresholds
